2604.11427.title: METRO: Towards Strategy Induction from Expert Dialogue Transcripts for Non-collaborative Dialogues
2604.11427.paper_summary: Developing non-collaborative dialogue agents traditionally requires the manual, unscalable codification of expert strategies. We propose \ours, a method that leverages large language models to autonomously induce both strategy actions and planning logic directly from raw transcripts. METRO formalizes expert knowledge into a Strategy Forest, a hierarchical structure that captures both short-term responses (nodes) and long-term strategic foresight (branches). Experimental results across two benchmarks show that METRO demonstrates promising performance, outperforming existing methods by an average of 9%-10%. Our further analysis not only reveals the success behind METRO (strategic behavioral diversity and foresight), but also demonstrates its robust cross-task transferability. This offers new insights into building non-collaborative agents in a cost-effective and scalable way. Our code is available at https://github.com/Humphrey-0125/METRO.
2604.11427.paper_summary_zh: 開發非協作對話代理通常需要手動且無法擴展的專家策略編碼。我們提出\ours，一種利用大型語言模型自動從原始逐字稿中引導策略行動和計劃邏輯的方法。METRO將專家知識形式化為策略樹，這是一種層次結構，捕捉短期反應（節點）和長期戰略前瞻（分支）。在兩個基準測試中的實驗結果顯示，METRO展現出令人鼓舞的表現，平均超越現有方法9%-10%。我們的進一步分析不僅揭示了METRO成功的原因（戰略行為多樣性和前瞻性），還展示了其強大的跨任務可轉移性。這為以成本效益和可擴展的方式構建非協作代理提供了新的見解。我們的代碼可在 https://github.com/Humphrey-0125/METRO 獲得。
2604.11427.author: Haofu Yang et.al.
2604.11427.authors: Haofu Yang, Jiaji Liu, Chen Huang, Faguo Wu, Wenqiang Lei, See-Kiong Ng
